children.title: 1,2,4-Triazoles: A Review of Synthetic Approaches and the Biological Activity
children.metadata: Triazole derivatives; Synthetic procedures; Biological activity; Drug design; Organic chemistry review
children.content: Suresh Maddila, Ramakanth Pagadala and Sreekanth B. Jonnalagadda*<|txt_split|>\* School of Chemistry & Physics, University of KwaZulu-Natal, Westville Campus, P Bag X 54001, Durban 4000, South Africa<|txt_split|>The review covers the latest literature on the synthetic procedures for various triazoles derivatives and their applications; the multipurpose synthetic applicability and biological activity of which will facilitate in planning and designing of new drugs.<|txt_split|>Letters in Organic Chemistry, Vol. 10, 2013, 715

children.title: Chemical Reaction Network
children.metadata: Chemical reaction diagram; arrows; central molecule; organic compounds
children.content: The image depicts a central molecule with multiple arrows pointing toward and away from it, representing various chemical reactions or transformations. Surrounding the central molecule are different organic compounds, each labeled with chemical structures and substituents such as R, R', X, and functional groups like -NH2, -COOH, -CN, and -NH-NH-. The arrows indicate the direction of reaction pathways, suggesting the central molecule is involved in or serves as a product of these reactions.

China<|txt_split|>A series of hydrazone derivatives were synthesized using conventional method and one-pot route, respectively.<|txt_split|>Letters in Organic Chemistry: Vol. 10: 2013, 722

children.title: Synthesis of Sulfur-Containing Heterocycles
children.metadata: Chemical reaction scheme; heterocyclic compounds; sulfur; nucleophilic substitution; reaction conditions
children.content: The image illustrates a multi-step synthetic pathway for the preparation of a sulfur-containing heterocyclic compound. It begins with compound 2, which contains a pyrrole-like ring with methyl substituents. The first step involves reacting compound 2 with H2NNHCNH2 in ethanol under reflux for 3 hours, yielding intermediate 4. In the second step, compound 4 is reacted with (A)CBr in ethanol under reflux for 4–20 minutes to produce a new compound. Finally, the same compound undergoes further reaction with H2NNHCNH2 in ethanol under reflux for 30–60 minutes to yield the final product, labeled as S-α, which contains a fused heterocyclic system with a sulfur atom. The reaction conditions and reagents are clearly annotated, and the structures are drawn with standard organic chemistry notation.

children.title: Tungstate Sulfuric Acid (TSA): A Green and Highly Efficient Catalyst for Novel and Known Polysubstituted Imidazoles Synthesis
children.metadata: Chemistry; Organic Chemistry; Iran; Yasouf University; Academic Publication
children.content: Bahador Karami*, Khalil Eskandari and Mahboobeh Azizi<|txt_split|>\*Department of Chemistry, P. O. Box 353, Yasouf University, Yasouf, 75918-7483, Iran<|txt_split|>Letters in Organic Chemistry, Vol. 10, 2013, 733

children.title: Solvent-Free Synthesis of Bicyclic Amines
children.metadata: organic synthesis, solvent-free reaction, catalytic system, bicyclic amines, transition metal catalyst
children.content: The image illustrates a solvent-free catalytic reaction scheme where a benzene derivative (left) reacts with a chlorobenzene derivative and an acid chloride under heat (Δ) in the presence of a Cu-based catalyst (Cu(OAc)₂) to form two distinct bicyclic amine products. The reaction proceeds via two pathways: one with R-NH₂ (ammonia derivative) yielding a substituted amine with a phenyl ring and a benzene ring, and another with NH₂OAcCH₃ (acetamide derivative) yielding a different substituted amine with a benzene ring and a heterocyclic ring. The catalyst is shown as a Cu(OAc)₂ complex in a solvent-free environment, with Δ indicating thermal conditions.

children.title: Efficient Synthesis of 6-O-methyl-scutellarein from Scutellarin via Selective Methylation
children.metadata: TCM Formulae Research; Jiangsu Key Laboratory; Organic Chemistry Letters; Chinese Medicine; Nanjing University of Chinese Medicine
children.content: Min-Zhe Shen\*, Zhi-Hao Shi\*, Nian-Guang Li\*, Hao-Tang, Qian-Ping Shi, Yu-Ping Tang\*, Jian-Ping Yang and Jin-Ao Duan\*<|txt_split|>* Jiangsu Key Laboratory for High Technology of TCM Formulae Research, Nanjing University of Chinese Medicine, Nanjing 210023, P.R. China<|txt_split|>Letters in Organic Chemistry, Vol. 10, 2013, 738

children.title: Chemical Transformation of Sennoside A to 6-O-Methyl-Sennoside
children.metadata: Chemical reaction; sennoside A; 6-O-methyl-sennoside; 7 steps; aromatic ring; glycoside
children.content: The image illustrates a chemical reaction pathway converting Sennoside A (compound 1) into 6-O-methyl-sennoside (compound 3) through a 7-step synthetic process. The left side shows Sennoside A, a complex molecule with a glucosylated structure attached to a xanthone core containing an aromatic ring with a phenolic hydroxyl group. The right side depicts 6-O-methyl-sennoside, which is structurally similar but features a methyl group at the 6-position of the glucose moiety. The reaction arrow labeled "7 steps" indicates the transformation involves multiple synthetic steps. Both molecules contain a phenolic -OH group on the aromatic ring, and the xanthone core structure remains largely intact, suggesting a selective modification at the glucose position.

children.title: Green and Facile Synthesis of 1,4-Disubstituted 1,2,3-Triazoles via a Click Reaction of \(\alpha\)-Bromo Ketones, \([\mathrm{bmin}]_{\mathrm{N}_{2}}\) and Terminal Acetylenes
children.metadata: Azadeh Fazeli; Hossein A. Oskooie; Yahya S. Beheshtiha; Majid M. Heravi; Hassan Valizadeh; Department of Chemistry; Alzahra University; Letters in Organic Chemistry; Vol. 10; 2013; 744
children.content: Azadeh Fazeli, Hossein A. Oskooie, Yahya S. Beheshtiha, Majid M. Heravi* and Hassan Valizadeh<|txt_split|>\(^{*}\) Department of Chemistry, School of Science, Alzahra University, Vanak, Tehran, Iran<|txt_split|>Letters in Organic Chemistry, Vol. 10: 2013, 744

children.title: Copper-Catalyzed Reaction Scheme
children.metadata: Organic synthesis; copper catalyst; CuCuSO₄; alkyne; nitrile; reaction conditions
children.content: The image displays a chemical reaction scheme where compounds 1a–e (aryl acetyl bromides) react with [BMIm]₂ (a bis(1-methylimidazolium) salt) and alkynes 3a–c to form compounds 4a–l under CuCuSO₄ catalysis in ethanol/water (1:1) at 0.5–2.5 h, with yields ranging from 75–95%. The structures are labeled with substituents such as Ph, 4-BrPh, 4-CH₃Ph, and 4-CH₃CH₂CH₃.

children.title: Efficient Green Synthesis of Bis(3-indolyl)phenylmethanes Catalyzed by 1-(Benzesulfonyl)-3- methyl-1H-imidazolium Chlorides
children.metadata: sulfonylimidazolium salts; green synthesis; bis(3-indolyl)phenylmethanes; electrophilic substitution; indoles; aldehydes; water catalysis
children.content: Gantigaiah Krishnamurthy* and Kesturu Venkatarayappa Jagannath<|txt_split|>\*Department of Studies in Chemistry, Central College Campus, Bangalore University, Bangalore, India<|txt_split|>An efficient sulfonylimidazolium salts catalyzed green synthesis of bis(3-indolyl)phenylmethanes by electrophilic substitution reaction of indoles with various aldehydes in water was reported.<|txt_split|>Letters in Organic Chemistry, Vol. 10, 2013, 752

children.title: Chemical Reaction Scheme
children.metadata: organic chemistry, reaction mechanism, synthesis, bromoarene coupling, water, heat
children.content: The image illustrates a chemical reaction scheme where an X-Br compound (a bromo-substituted benzene derivative) reacts with an aryl carbonyl compound (R-C(=O)–) in the presence of a chlorinated aromatic compound (containing Cl atoms and a sulfonyl group) under aqueous conditions and heat. The reaction proceeds to form a complex tricyclic aromatic compound with fused benzene rings and nitrogen heterocycles, suggesting a multicomponent coupling reaction.

children.title: Novel 1,2,3-Triazole-Bisphenol-Based Macrocycle Synthesized Through Click Chemistry
children.metadata: novel flexible macrocycle; click chemistry synthesis; meal capture; organic chemistry letters
children.content: Burhan Khan, Said Nadeem*, Muhammad R. Shah, Sammer Yousuf and Numan Hoda<|txt_split|>\* International Center for Chemical and Biological Sciences, HEJ Research Institute of Chemistry, University of Karachi, Karachi-75270, Pakistan and Department of Chemistry, Alendz University, 07058 Antalya, Turkey<|txt_split|>Novel flexible macrocycle for capturing meals of different sized.<|txt_split|>Novel macrocycle synthesized by Click Chemistry<|txt_split|>Letters in Organic Chemistry; Vol. 10; 2013, 758

children.title: Chemical Reaction Scheme
children.metadata: organic synthesis, coupling reaction, triphenylmethane derivative, copper catalyst, solvent system
children.content: The image depicts a chemical reaction scheme involving the coupling of two triphenylmethane derivatives with terminal alkynes. The reactants are shown as two symmetric molecules, each featuring a central phenyl ring flanked by two phenyl rings connected via ether bridges to ethyl-terminated nitrile groups, and each also bearing a terminal alkyne group. The reaction conditions are specified as 10 mol% AgOAc, 0.5 mol% CuSO4·H2O, in the presence of KHCDS and DMF at 60°C for 3 hours. The arrow indicates the reaction direction, implying the formation of a new bond between the two molecules, likely resulting in a conjugated triphenylmethane derivative with extended conjugation.

children.title: Chemical Structure of a Organic Molecule
children.metadata: Organic molecule; aromatic rings; azo groups; ether linkages; cyclic structure
children.content: The image displays a cyclic organic molecule composed of two benzene rings connected via an azo group (–N=N–) and ether linkages (–O–). The structure forms a ring with alternating phenyl and azo units, and each phenyl ring is further connected to a chain containing an ether oxygen and a terminal phenyl group. The molecule features a symmetric arrangement with two identical halves, each containing a phenyl ring linked to a chain terminating in an ether oxygen, and the central azo group connecting the two halves.

children.title: Molecular Structure Diagram
children.metadata: Chemical structure; organic molecule; benzene ring; carbon atoms; hydrogen atoms; functional groups
children.content: The image displays a detailed molecular structure diagram of an organic compound, featuring a long chain of interconnected benzene rings and various functional groups. The structure includes multiple carbon atoms bonded in a linear fashion, with hydrogen atoms attached to each carbon. The molecule terminates with two branched side chains, each terminating in a spherical representation of a functional group. The diagram uses standard chemical notation with atoms labeled and bonds shown as lines, illustrating the three-dimensional spatial arrangement of the molecule.

children.title: Copper- and Solvent-free Sonogashira Reaction Catalyzed by Polyvinyl Chloride-supported Palladium(II) Triazole Complex
children.metadata: Copper-free coupling; Solvent-free coupling; Palladium triazole complex; Polyvinyl chloride-supported catalyst; Aryl halide-alkyne reaction
children.content: Mohammad Bakherad*, Ali Keivanloo, Bahram Bahramian, and Maesoume Siavoshi<|txt_split|>\* School of Chemistry, Shahrood University of Technology, Shahrood, Iran<|txt_split|>Copper- and solvent-free coupling reaction of various aryl halides and terminal alkynes catalyzed by polyvinyl chloride-supported palladium (II) triazole complex.<|txt_split|>Letters in Organic Chemistry; Vol. 10, 2013, 764

children.title: Chemical Reaction Scheme
children.metadata: organic chemistry, reaction mechanism, PVC, thiourea, [ICl2]·[PCNCl2], solvent, reaction conditions
children.content: The image illustrates a two-step chemical reaction scheme. In the first step, a thiourea derivative (with methyl and amino substituents) reacts with chlorinated PVC under DMF solvent at 80°C for 20 hours, forming an intermediate where the thiourea is attached to a PVC chain. In the second step, this intermediate is treated with [ICl2]·[PCNCl2] in the presence of BOH and refluxed for 15 hours, resulting in a final product where the thiourea is bonded to the PVC chain, and the reaction introduces a phosphorus-containing group with chloride substituents.

children.title: Green and Highly Efficient One-Pot Synthesis of Some New Bis Thiazolidinones
children.metadata: bis thiazolidinones; deep eutectic solvents; one-pot synthesis; environmentally friendly; highly efficient
children.content: Akbar Mobinikhaledi and Alireza Khajeh Amiri*<|txt_split|>\*Department of Chemistry, Faculty of Science, Arak University, Arak 38156-8-8349, Iran<|txt_split|>In this study, an environmentally friendly, highly efficient and facile procedure for one-pot synthesis of bis thiazolidinones in the presence of deep eutectic solvents (DES) has been described.<|txt_split|>Letters in Organic Chemistry, Vol. 10, 2013, 770

children.title: Chemical Reaction Scheme
children.metadata: organic synthesis, reaction mechanism, aromatic compounds, electrophilic substitution
children.content: The image displays a chemical reaction scheme involving the synthesis of various substituted benzene derivatives. Starting from the left, two reactants are shown: an aluminum-based compound and a compound containing a double bond. The reaction proceeds under specific conditions (CHCl₃/70°C, 60-120 min) to form intermediates including a chlorobenzene derivative and a substituted benzene. The scheme then branches into multiple pathways, leading to different products such as aromatic compounds with nitrogen, oxygen, and sulfur atoms, indicating the formation of various substituted benzene derivatives through electrophilic substitution reactions.

children.title: Synthesis of Methylenebisamides by TFA-Promoted Reactions of Nitriles and DMSO
children.metadata: Methylenebisamides; TFA-promoted reaction; Nitriles; DMSO; Synthesis
children.content: Lingxia Pan, Lin Huang and Chunsong Xie*<|txt_split|>\* College of Material, Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Hangzhou Normal University, Hangzhou 310036, P.R. China<|txt_split|>Synthesis of Methylenebisamides by TFA-Promoted Reactions of Nitriles and DMSO

children.title: Chemical Reaction for Urethane Formation
children.metadata: Chemical reaction; R-CN, DMSO, TFA, 95°C, 12 h, Aryl-2-Pyridyl
children.content: The image displays a chemical reaction scheme where an aryl-2-pyridyl nitrile (R-CN) reacts with DMSO in the presence of trifluoroacetic acid (TFA) under heating conditions of 95°C for 12 hours, producing a urethane derivative with the structure R–C(=O)–NH–NH–C(=O)–R. The reaction conditions are explicitly noted above the arrow, and the substituent R is specified as "Aryl-2-Pyridyl" below the reactant.
